background: In a single-cell massive MIMO system operating in Time-Division Duplexing (TDD) mode, the uplink sum rate characterizes the total data rate achievable by all users. The system employs a zero-forcing receiver at the base station and max-min fairness power control. Here, $R_{\text{sum}}$ is the uplink sum rate (in bits/second), $K$ is the number of single-antenna users (dimensionless), $M$ is the number of base station antennas (dimensionless, with $M > K$), $B$ is the system bandwidth (in Hz), $B_c$ is the channel coherence bandwidth (in Hz), $T_c$ is the channel coherence time (in seconds), $\mathrm{SNR}$ is the signal-to-noise ratio (dimensionless linear power ratio), $\beta_k$ is the path loss for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio), and $\gamma_k$ is the mean-square channel estimate for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio, with $0 < \gamma_k \leq \beta_k$).
question_text: Fill in the missing signal-to-noise ratio parameter and the summation term that accounts for multi-user interference.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a single-cell massive MIMO system operating in Time-Division Duplexing (TDD) mode, the uplink sum rate characterizes the total data rate achievable by all users. The system employs a zero-forcing receiver at the base station and max-min fairness power control. Here, $R_{\text{sum}}$ is the uplink sum rate (in bits/second), $K$ is the number of single-antenna users (dimensionless), $M$ is the number of base station antennas (dimensionless, with $M > K$), $B$ is the system bandwidth (in Hz), $B_c$ is the channel coherence bandwidth (in Hz), $T_c$ is the channel coherence time (in seconds), $\mathrm{SNR}$ is the signal-to-noise ratio (dimensionless linear power ratio), $\beta_k$ is the path loss for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio), and $\gamma_k$ is the mean-square channel estimate for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio, with $0 < \gamma_k \leq \beta_k$).
question_text: What term representing the channel estimation error impact is missing from the SINR denominator?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the same RIS-assisted MU-MIMO system, the spectral efficiency measures the achievable rate for each user. The expression is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) at the receiver, where $\mathsf{SE}_k$ represents the spectral efficiency for user $k$ (in bits/s/Hz), $\mathbf{H}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the direct BS-user channel, $\mathbf{G}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times M}$ is the RIS-user channel, $\boldsymbol{\Theta} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times M}$ is the RIS phase-shift matrix, $\mathbf{H}_{BR} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times N_t}$ is the BS-RIS channel, $\mathbf{f}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for the desired user $k$, $\mathbf{f}_u \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ are beamforming vectors for interfering users ($u \neq k$), and $\sigma_k^2$ is the noise variance at user $k$ (in watts).
question_text: Fill in the numerator (desired signal power) and the interference term.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a single-cell massive MIMO system operating in Time-Division Duplexing (TDD) mode, the uplink sum rate characterizes the total data rate achievable by all users. The system employs a zero-forcing receiver at the base station and max-min fairness power control. Here, $R_{\text{sum}}$ is the uplink sum rate (in bits/second), $K$ is the number of single-antenna users (dimensionless), $M$ is the number of base station antennas (dimensionless, with $M > K$), $B$ is the system bandwidth (in Hz), $B_c$ is the channel coherence bandwidth (in Hz), $T_c$ is the channel coherence time (in seconds), $\mathrm{SNR}$ is the signal-to-noise ratio (dimensionless linear power ratio), $\beta_k$ is the path loss for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio), and $\gamma_k$ is the mean-square channel estimate for user $k$ (dimensionless linear power ratio, with $0 < \gamma_k \leq \beta_k$).

background: In a Simultaneously Transmitting and Reflecting Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (STAR-RIS)-enabled multi-user MIMO Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) system, a dual-function base station (BS) serves communication users while sensing targets. The post-combining signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) for a specific target is derived to quantify sensing performance. In this context, $\gamma_{\mathrm{s}l}(\mathbf{J}, \boldsymbol{\varphi}_l)$ is the SINR for sensing target $l$, $\bar{\alpha}$ is the expected radar cross-section (RCS) of the targets (dimensionless), $\boldsymbol{\varphi}_l \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times 1}$ is the unit-norm receive beamforming vector at the BS for target $l$ ($\Vert \boldsymbol{\varphi}_l \Vert^2 = 1$), $\mathbf{V}_{\mathrm{R}l} = \mathbf{v}_\mathrm{R}(\phi_l) \mathbf{v}_\mathrm{T}(\phi_l) \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the target response matrix for target $l$ (combining transmit and receive steering vectors), $\boldsymbol{\Sigma} = \sum_{k \in \mathcal{K}} \mathbf{J}_{\mathrm{c}k} + \sum_{l' \in \mathcal{L}} \mathbf{J}_{\mathrm{s}l'} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{T} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the total transmit covariance matrix, $\mathbf{G} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the self-interference channel matrix at the BS, and the constant $1$ in the denominator represents the normalized noise power.
question_text: Fill in the two missing interference covariance terms in the denominator.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a goal-oriented edge inferencing system, an end device offloads data to a Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) Host. The uplink transmission delay models the time required to send the data over a wireless channel. The equation is given by $D_k^u = [MASK]$, where $D_k^u$ is the uplink transmission delay for device $k$ (in seconds), $n_k$ is the data size to be transmitted (in bits), $B_k^u$ is the allocated uplink bandwidth (in Hz), $\mathrm{BER}_k$ is the target bit error rate (dimensionless), $h_k^u$ is the channel power gain (dimensionless), $p_k^u$ is the device's transmit power (in watts), and $N_0$ is the noise power spectral density (in W/Hz). The function $\varphi(\mathrm{BER}_k) = \frac{-1.5}{\ln(5\cdot\mathrm{BER}_k)}$ is the BER margin, an approximation valid for spectral efficiency $\geq 4$.
question_text: Fill in the channel power gain variable and the complete denominator of the SNR fraction.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the same RIS-assisted MU-MIMO system, the spectral efficiency measures the achievable rate for each user. The expression is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) at the receiver, where $\mathsf{SE}_k$ represents the spectral efficiency for user $k$ (in bits/s/Hz), $\mathbf{H}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the direct BS-user channel, $\mathbf{G}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times M}$ is the RIS-user channel, $\boldsymbol{\Theta} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times M}$ is the RIS phase-shift matrix, $\mathbf{H}_{BR} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times N_t}$ is the BS-RIS channel, $\mathbf{f}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for the desired user $k$, $\mathbf{f}_u \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ are beamforming vectors for interfering users ($u \neq k$), and $\sigma_k^2$ is the noise variance at user $k$ (in watts).
question_text: What term represents the total interference power from other users?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a joint THz/mmWave 6G system, the downlink signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) is a key performance metric. For the mmWave link, the SINR at a user equipment (UE) located at a 3D distance $y$ from its serving base station (BS) depends on transmit power, antenna gains, path loss, and blockage conditions. Specifically, $S(y)$ is the SINR (dimensionless), $P_M$ is the mmWave BS transmit power (in watts), $G_{M,B}$ is the gain of the mmWave BS antenna array (dimensionless), $G_{M,U}$ is the gain of the UE antenna array when connected to the mmWave BS (dimensionless), $y$ is the 3D distance between the UE and the mmWave BS (in meters), $\zeta_{M,1}=2.1$ is the path loss exponent for a non-blocked (LoS) mmWave link (dimensionless), $\zeta_{M,2}=3.19$ is the path loss exponent for a blocked (NLoS) mmWave link (dimensionless), $p_B(y)$ is the probability that a link of length $y$ is blocked (domain $[0,1]$), $A_M = 10^{2 \log_{10} f_{M,c} + 3.24}$ is a propagation coefficient that incorporates the carrier frequency $f_{M,c}$ (in Hz), $N_0$ is the thermal noise power spectral density (in W/Hz), $B_M$ is the mmWave system bandwidth (in Hz), and $M_{M,I}$ is an interference margin for the mmWave link (in dB).
question_text: What probability term weights the blocked (NLoS) path loss component?
equation: $$S(y) = \frac{P_M G_{M,B} G_{M,U} \left[ y^{-\zeta_{M,1}} [1 - p_B(y)] + y^{-\zeta_{M,2}} [MASK] \right]}{A_M (N_0 B_M + M_{M,I})}$$
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: This equation models the achievable data rate $\mathcal{R}_d^i$ (in bits per second) at the end-user in time slot $i$. The rate depends on the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of the combined backscattered and actively transmitted signals from the UAV. Key parameters include: $B$, the system bandwidth (in Hz); $e^{-E}$, a constant involving Euler's constant $E$ (dimensionless); $\omega_0$, the reference channel gain (dimensionless); $\zeta_i$, the Doppler factor (dimensionless); $\eta^i$, the backscatter coefficient in slot $i$ (dimensionless); $P_s$, the GBS's data transmit power (in W); $\bar{P}_u$, the effective cached data transmit power from the UAV (in W); $d_{su}^i$ and $d_{du}^i$, the distances from GBS to UAV and from UAV to end-user, respectively (in m); $\alpha$, the path-loss exponent; and $\tilde{\sigma}_d^i$, the effective noise variance at the end-user (in W).
question_text: Fill in the two signal power terms inside the numerator's parentheses.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In this downlink RIS-assisted MU-MIMO system, the performance for each user is characterized by its Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR). The SINR quantifies the strength of the desired signal relative to the interference from other users and the background noise. Here, $\mathrm{SINR}_{k}$ is the SINR for user $k$ (unitless), $\mathbf{h}_{2,k} \in \mathbb{C}^{1 \times N}$ is the channel vector from the RIS to user $k$, $\pmb{\Phi} \in \mathbb{C}^{N \times N}$ is the RIS phase-shift matrix, $\mathbf{H}_1 \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times N}$ is the channel matrix from the BS to the RIS, $\mathbf{w}_{k} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for user $k$, $\mathbf{w}_{i} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for an interfering user $i$, and $\sigma_{k}^{2}$ is the noise power at user $k$ (in watts).
question_text: What is the composite effective channel from the BS to user $k$?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the HAPC-enabled SR network, the achievable backscatter communication (BC) rate for an A-IoT device $k$ during the mutualism phase of duration $\tau_0$ is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of its backscattered signal. In this equation, $R_k^{BC}$ is the achievable rate of device $k$ in bits per second (bps), $\tau_0$ is the duration of the mutualism phase (in seconds, $0 \leq \tau_0 \leq 1$), $B$ is the channel bandwidth (in Hz), $P_p$ is the transmit power of the ambient source (in W), $|\Gamma_k|$ is the magnitude of the complex backscatter coefficient (dimensionless), $|h_k|$ is the magnitude of the channel from the ambient source to device $k$, $|g_k|$ is the magnitude of the channel from device $k$ to the receiver, $|h_p|$ is the magnitude of the direct channel from the ambient source to the receiver, and $\sigma^2$ is the noise power (in W).
question_text: Write the complete achievable rate equation for device $k$ in the mutualism phase.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the HAPC-enabled SR network, the achievable backscatter communication (BC) rate for an A-IoT device $k$ during the mutualism phase of duration $\tau_0$ is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) of its backscattered signal. In this equation, $R_k^{BC}$ is the achievable rate of device $k$ in bits per second (bps), $\tau_0$ is the duration of the mutualism phase (in seconds, $0 \leq \tau_0 \leq 1$), $B$ is the channel bandwidth (in Hz), $P_p$ is the transmit power of the ambient source (in W), $|\Gamma_k|$ is the magnitude of the complex backscatter coefficient (dimensionless), $|h_k|$ is the magnitude of the channel from the ambient source to device $k$, $|g_k|$ is the magnitude of the channel from device $k$ to the receiver, $|h_p|$ is the magnitude of the direct channel from the ambient source to the receiver, and $\sigma^2$ is the noise power (in W).
question_text: Fill in the backscatter coefficient and the channel from the device to the receiver.
correct_answer: \boxed{\Gamma_k} and \boxed{g_k} (The equation expects a single term, so the answer is the product $\Gamma_k g_k$)
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the same mmWave downlink system, the spectral efficiency for each user is calculated based on the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), where $R_k \in \mathbb{R}^+$ is the spectral efficiency for user $k$ (in bits/s/Hz), $\log_2(\cdot)$ is the base-2 logarithm, $P_T \in \mathbb{R}^+$ is the total transmit power (in watts), $K \in \mathbb{Z}^+$ is the number of users, $\boldsymbol{h}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N \times 1}$ is the channel vector to user $k$, $\boldsymbol{f}_{m,k} \in \mathbb{C}^{N \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for user $k$, $\sigma^2 \in \mathbb{R}^+$ is the noise variance (in watts), and $\lVert \cdot \rVert^2$ denotes the squared magnitude.
question_text: Fill in the three missing coefficients for desired power, interference power, and noise.
equation: $$R _ { k } = \log _ { 2 } \left( 1 + \frac { [MASK] \left\lVert \boldsymbol{h} _ { k } ^ { T } \boldsymbol{f} _ { m , k } \right\rVert ^ { 2 } } { [MASK] \left\lVert \boldsymbol{h} _ { k } ^ { T } \boldsymbol{f} _ { m , k ^ { \prime } } \right\rVert ^ { 2 } + [MASK] } \right)$$
correct_answer: \boxed{\frac { P _ { T } } { K }}, \boxed{\sum _ { \substack { k ^ { \prime } \in \mathcal{K} \\ k ^ { \prime } \ne k } } \frac { P _ { T } } { K }}, and \boxed{\sigma ^ { 2 }}
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a multi-user Single-Input Multiple-Output (SIMO) OTFS system, the Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) for a given user $k$ is a key performance metric. Here, $\Upsilon_k$ is the SINR for the $k$-th user (dimensionless), $E_s$ is the average symbol energy (in Joules), $N_0$ is the noise power spectral density (in W/Hz), $K_u$ is the total number of users, $h_p^{(k')}$ is the complex channel gain for the $p$-th path of the $k'$-th user, and $|h_p^{(k')}|^2$ is its squared magnitude representing the path power. The denominator $Z_k$ separates the constant noise term from the aggregate interference power $S = \frac{E_s}{N_0} \sum_{k' = k + 1}^{K_u} \sum_{p = 1}^{P} |h_p^{(k')}|^2$ from all subsequent users.
question_text: Write the complete equation defining the normalized denominator $Z_k$ of the SINR.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In an Open RAN (O-RAN) system with a single-antenna Radio Unit (RU), the achievable data rate for a user equipment (UE) is calculated across all allocated Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs). The system serves multiple UEs belonging to eMBB, URLLC, or mMTC network slices with distinct QoS requirements. Here, $R_k \in \mathbb{R}^{+}$ is the aggregate data rate for UE $k$ (in bps), $B \in \mathbb{Z}^{+}$ is the total number of available PRBs (dimensionless), $\delta_{k,b} \in \{0, 1\}$ is the binary PRB allocation indicator (1 if PRB $b$ is allocated to UE $k$, 0 otherwise; dimensionless), $P_k \in \mathbb{R}^{+}$ is the transmit power allocated to UE $k$ (in W or dBm), $g_k \in \mathbb{R}^{+}$ is the channel gain for UE $k$ (dimensionless, linear scale), $\sigma^2 \in \mathbb{R}^{+}$ is the noise power (in W), and $I \in \mathbb{R}^{+}$ is the inter-cell interference power (in W).
question_text: Write the complete equation for the aggregate data rate of UE $k$ across all PRBs.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a VLC-NOMA system, users are grouped into clusters served by the same AP. Within a cluster, users are ordered by their channel power gain for Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC). The Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) determines a user's data rate. For user $u_k$ (the $k$-th strongest user in the cluster of AP $a$ at time $t$), $\mathrm{SINR}_{a,u_k}(t)$ is the SINR (dimensionless), $G_{a,u_k}(t) = (R H_{a,u}(t))^2$ is the channel power gain (dimensionless) where $R$ is the photodiode responsivity (in A/W), $P_{a,u_k}(t)$ is the electrical power allocated to user $u_k$ from AP $a$ (in W), $K_a(t)$ is the number of users in the cluster of AP $a$, $I_{\mathrm{ICI},u_k}(t)$ is the inter-cell interference power from all other APs (in W), and $\sigma_{\mathrm{noise}}^2$ is the noise variance (in W). The denominator includes intra-cluster interference from users with stronger channels (which should be cancelled by SIC) and weaker channels (which are treated as noise), plus inter-cell interference and noise.
question_text: Write the complete SINR equation for the $k$-th strongest user in a NOMA cluster.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a VLC-NOMA system, users are grouped into clusters served by the same AP. Within a cluster, users are ordered by their channel power gain for Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC). The Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) determines a user's data rate. For user $u_k$ (the $k$-th strongest user in the cluster of AP $a$ at time $t$), $\mathrm{SINR}_{a,u_k}(t)$ is the SINR (dimensionless), $G_{a,u_k}(t) = (R H_{a,u}(t))^2$ is the channel power gain (dimensionless) where $R$ is the photodiode responsivity (in A/W), $P_{a,u_k}(t)$ is the electrical power allocated to user $u_k$ from AP $a$ (in W), $K_a(t)$ is the number of users in the cluster of AP $a$, $I_{\mathrm{ICI},u_k}(t)$ is the inter-cell interference power from all other APs (in W), and $\sigma_{\mathrm{noise}}^2$ is the noise variance (in W). The denominator includes intra-cluster interference from users with stronger channels (which should be cancelled by SIC) and weaker channels (which are treated as noise), plus inter-cell interference and noise.
question_text: Fill in the two types of interference experienced by the user.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: The optimal power allocation for communication UEs in a multi-user MIMO system is derived using the Karush-Kuhn-Tucker (KKT) conditions to solve the constrained optimization problem. This solution follows a water-filling structure. Here, $P_{C,k,i}$ is the optimal power allocated to the $i$-th sub-channel of communication UE $k$ (in W), $[\cdot]^+ = \max(0, \cdot)$ ensures non-negative power, $B$ is the bandwidth (in Hz), $\nu_{k,i}$ is the Lagrange multiplier associated with the minimum capacity constraint for the $i$-th sub-channel of UE $k$ (dimensionless), $\mu$ is the Lagrange multiplier associated with the total power constraint (in W$^{-1}$), $N_{0,k}$ is the noise power at UE $k$ (in W), $I_{k,i}$ is the interference power (in W), and $\lambda_{k,i}$ is the channel gain eigenvalue (dimensionless).
question_text: Write the complete optimal power allocation formula for a communication user equipment (UE).
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: At the access point (AP), the signal from each user is decoded using a linear receiver and successive interference cancellation (SIC). The signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) for a user determines its achievable rate and depends on the receive beamforming vector, channel gains, and interference from other users, where $\gamma_k$ is the SINR for user $k$ at the AP (dimensionless), $p_k$ is the transmit power of user $k$ (in watts), $\mathbf{w}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_a \times 1}$ is the AP's detection vector for user $k$ ($\|\mathbf{w}_k\|^2 = 1$), $\mathbf{h}_j \in \mathbb{C}^{N_a \times 1}$ is the equivalent channel from user $j$ to the AP, and $\sigma_a^2$ is the noise variance at the AP (in watts). The SIC decoding order is based on channel strengths ($\|\mathbf{h}_1\| > \|\mathbf{h}_2\| > \dots > \|\mathbf{h}_K\|$), meaning users decoded later experience interference from users not yet decoded.
question_text: Fill in the two terms in the denominator: the interference power and the noise power.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: The signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) is a key metric for evaluating user performance in multiuser MIMO systems, where $\mathrm{SINR}_k$ represents the SINR for the $k$-th user (dimensionless), $P$ is the total transmitted power (in watts), $K$ is the number of users, $\mathbf{w}_{\mathrm{RF}_k} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times 1}$ is the analog combiner vector for user $k$ with elements from discrete set $\mathcal{W}$, $w_{\mathrm{BB}_k} \in \mathbb{C}$ is the baseband equalizer scalar for user $k$, $\mathbf{H}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the channel matrix for user $k$, $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{RF}} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N_t^{\mathrm{RF}}}$ is the analog precoder matrix, $\mathbf{f}_{\mathrm{BB}_j} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t^{\mathrm{RF}} \times 1}$ is the $j$-th column of the baseband precoder matrix $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{BB}}$ (representing the baseband precoder for user $j$), $\sigma^2$ is the noise variance (in watts), and $\|\cdot\|_2$ denotes the Euclidean norm.
question_text: Identify the two components that constitute the denominator.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a cellular downlink system with OFDMA resource allocation, high-bandwidth (HB) user equipment (UEs) share resource blocks (RBs) with other services. The downlink rate for a specific HB UE is determined by its channel quality and allocated resources, where $r_e^{(t)}$ is the instantaneous data rate for HB UE $e$ at time step $t$ (in bit/s), $K_e^{(t)} \in \mathbb{N}$ is the number of RBs allocated to HB UE $e$ at time $t$ (dimensionless), $B$ is the bandwidth of a single RB (in Hz), $\gamma_e^{(t)}$ is the power-normalized signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) for HB UE $e$ at time $t$ (in W$^{-1}$), $P^{(d)}$ is the base station transmit power per RB (in W), $h_e^{(t)}$ is the channel gain between the base station and HB UE $e$ at time $t$ (dimensionless), and $N_0$ is the noise spectral density (in W/Hz).
question_text: Write the complete expression for the instantaneous downlink rate of an HB UE.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a cellular downlink system with OFDMA resource allocation, high-bandwidth (HB) user equipment (UEs) share resource blocks (RBs) with other services. background: In a Simultaneously Transmitting and Reflecting Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (STAR-RIS)-enabled multi-user MIMO Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) system, a dual-function base station (BS) serves communication users while sensing targets. The post-combining signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) for a specific target is derived to quantify sensing performance. In this context, $\gamma_{\mathrm{s}l}(\mathbf{J}, \boldsymbol{\varphi}_l)$ is the SINR for sensing target $l$, $\bar{\alpha}$ is the expected radar cross-section (RCS) of the targets (dimensionless), $\boldsymbol{\varphi}_l \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times 1}$ is the unit-norm receive beamforming vector at the BS for target $l$ ($\Vert \boldsymbol{\varphi}_l \Vert^2 = 1$), $\mathbf{V}_{\mathrm{R}l} = \mathbf{v}_\mathrm{R}(\phi_l) \mathbf{v}_\mathrm{T}(\phi_l) \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the target response matrix for target $l$ (combining transmit and receive steering vectors), $\boldsymbol{\Sigma} = \sum_{k \in \mathcal{K}} \mathbf{J}_{\mathrm{c}k} + \sum_{l' \in \mathcal{L}} \mathbf{J}_{\mathrm{s}l'} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{T} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the total transmit covariance matrix, $\mathbf{G} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_\mathrm{R} \times N_\mathrm{T}}$ is the self-interference channel matrix at the BS, and the constant $1$ in the denominator represents the normalized noise power.
question_text: Which expression correctly represents the sensing SINR for target $l$ that should replace [MASK]?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the multi-hop RIS-assisted system, the achievable rate for each user is determined by its Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR). The SINR calculation separates the desired signal power from the same-user beamformer from the interference power caused by other users' beamformers and the additive noise. Here, $\rho_k$ is the SINR for user $k$ (dimensionless), $\mathbf{g}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{I_k} \times 1}$ is the channel vector from the last RIS to user $k$, $\mathbf{\Phi}_i \in \mathbb{C}^{N_i \times N_i}$ is the phase-shift matrix of the $i$-th RIS, $\mathbf{H}_i \in \mathbb{C}^{N_i \times N_{i-1}}$ is the inter-RIS or BS-RIS channel matrix, $\mathbf{w}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{1 \times M}$ is the direct BS-user channel vector, $\mathbf{f}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for user $k$, $s_j \sim \mathcal{CN}(0,1)$ is the data symbol for user $j$ (unitless), and $\sigma_n^2$ is the noise variance at the user (in watts).
question_text: What is the summation term for the interference from other users?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the wideband MIMO-OFDM system with hybrid beamforming, the spectral efficiency quantifies the achievable data rate across all subcarriers. Here, $\mathcal{R}$ represents the spectral efficiency (in bits/s/Hz), $K$ is the number of OFDM subcarriers, $\det(\cdot)$ denotes the determinant operator, $\mathbf{I}_{N_s}$ is the identity matrix of size $N_s \times N_s$, $N_s$ is the number of data streams, $P_r$ is the average received power (in watts), $\sigma_n^2$ is the noise variance (in watts), $\mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{BB}}^{*}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^r \times N_s}$ is the conjugate transpose of the digital baseband combiner, $\mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{RF}}^{*} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^r \times N_r}$ is the conjugate transpose of the analog RF combiner, $\mathbf{H}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the channel matrix at subcarrier $k$, $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{RF}} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N_{\mathrm{RF}}^t}$ is the analog RF precoder, and $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{BB}}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^t \times N_s}$ is the digital baseband precoder.
question_text: Complete the five missing components: digital combiner, analog combiner, analog precoder, digital precoder, and analog combiner.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the wideband MIMO-OFDM system with hybrid beamforming, the spectral efficiency quantifies the achievable data rate across all subcarriers. Here, $\mathcal{R}$ represents the spectral efficiency (in bits/s/Hz), $K$ is the number of OFDM subcarriers, $\det(\cdot)$ denotes the determinant operator, $\mathbf{I}_{N_s}$ is the identity matrix of size $N_s \times N_s$, $N_s$ is the number of data streams, $P_r$ is the average received power (in watts), $\sigma_n^2$ is the noise variance (in watts), $\mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{BB}}^{*}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^r \times N_s}$ is the conjugate transpose of the digital baseband combiner, $\mathbf{W}_{\mathrm{RF}}^{*} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^r \times N_r}$ is the conjugate transpose of the analog RF combiner, $\mathbf{H}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the channel matrix at subcarrier $k$, $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{RF}} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N_{\mathrm{RF}}^t}$ is the analog RF precoder, and $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{BB}}[k] \in \mathbb{C}^{N_{\mathrm{RF}}^t \times N_s}$ is the digital baseband precoder.
question_text: Which expression correctly represents the effective signal-to-noise ratio matrix that should replace [MASK]?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In URLLC systems with statistical channel knowledge, the number of resource units (RUs) needed for successful transmission is calculated based on channel conditions and reliability requirements. Here, $\mathcal{F}(c,i,\rho)$ represents the number of RUs required for device $i$ on channel $c$ to achieve reliability $\rho$ (dimensionless), $\ell$ is the packet size (in bits), $q = B\tau$ is the number of channel uses per RU where $B$ is bandwidth (Hz) and $\tau$ is RU duration (s) (dimensionless), $\Gamma_T$ is the transmit SNR (dimensionless), $\rho$ is the target reliability (dimensionless), $\Lambda_c$ is the interference factor on channel $c$ (dimensionless), $d_i$ is the distance from device $i$ to the access point (in meters), and $\alpha$ is the path loss exponent (dimensionless).
question_text: Fill in the transmit power term and the path loss component.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: The signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) is a key performance metric for downlink communication users in the ISAC system. It quantifies the strength of the desired signal relative to interference and noise at the receiver, where $\gamma_k^{\mathrm{DL}}$ is the downlink SINR for user $k$ (unitless), $\mathbf{h}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the downlink channel vector to user $k$, $\mathbf{f}_i \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for user $i$, $\mathbf{S}_m \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N_t}$ is the covariance matrix for the sensing signal intended for target $m$, $\sigma_k^2$ is the noise power at user $k$'s receiver (in watts), $K$ is the number of downlink users (unitless), and $M$ is the number of targets (unitless). The numerator represents the desired signal power, while the denominator comprises multi-user interference, sensing interference, and noise.
question_text: What term accounts for the interference caused by the dedicated sensing signals?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In massive MIMO systems, the channel capacity serves as a key performance metric that captures the maximum achievable data rate under specific encoding and decoding strategies. The effective capacity accounts for the combined effects of the hybrid precoding/combining architecture and the noise characteristics, where $C$ represents the channel capacity (in bits/sec/Hz), $\mathbf{I}_K$ is the $K \times K$ identity matrix ($K$ is the number of data streams), $\rho$ is the average received power (in watts), $N$ is the number of transmitted data streams, $\mathbf{R}_n = \sigma_n^2 \mathbf{W} \mathbf{W}^H$ is the noise covariance matrix after combining ($\sigma_n^2$ is the noise variance), $\mathbf{W} = \tilde{\mathbf{D}}\tilde{\mathbf{A}} \in \mathbb{C}^{K \times N_r}$ is the full combining matrix, $\mathbf{H} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the channel matrix, $\mathbf{A} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N}$ is the analog precoding matrix, and $\mathbf{D} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times N}$ is the digital precoding matrix.
question_text: Write the complete channel capacity equation for the hybrid massive MIMO system.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In massive MIMO systems, the channel capacity serves as a key performance metric that captures the maximum achievable data rate under specific encoding and decoding strategies. The effective capacity accounts for the combined effects of the hybrid precoding/combining architecture and the noise characteristics, where $C$ represents the channel capacity (in bits/sec/Hz), $\mathbf{I}_K$ is the $K \times K$ identity matrix ($K$ is the number of data streams), $\rho$ is the average received power (in watts), $N$ is the number of transmitted data streams, $\mathbf{R}_n = \sigma_n^2 \mathbf{W} \mathbf{W}^H$ is the noise covariance matrix after combining ($\sigma_n^2$ is the noise variance), $\mathbf{W} = \tilde{\mathbf{D}}\tilde{\mathbf{A}} \in \mathbb{C}^{K \times N_r}$ is the full combining matrix, $\mathbf{H} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the channel matrix, $\mathbf{A} \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times N}$ is the analog precoding matrix, and $\mathbf{D} \in \mathbb{R}^{N \times N}$ is the digital precoding matrix.
question_text: What matrix accounts for the colored noise after combining?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the same RIS-assisted MU-MIMO system, the spectral efficiency measures the achievable rate for each user. The expression is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) at the receiver, where $\mathsf{SE}_k$ represents the spectral efficiency for user $k$ (in bits/s/Hz), $\mathbf{H}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the direct BS-user channel, $\mathbf{G}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times M}$ is the RIS-user channel, $\boldsymbol{\Theta} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times M}$ is the RIS phase-shift matrix, $\mathbf{H}_{BR} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times N_t}$ is the BS-RIS channel, $\mathbf{f}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for the desired user $k$, $\mathbf{f}_u \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ are beamforming vectors for interfering users ($u \neq k$), and $\sigma_k^2$ is the noise variance at user $k$ (in watts).
question_text: Complete the four missing components describing the combined channel.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the same RIS-assisted MU-MIMO system, the spectral efficiency measures the achievable rate for each user. The expression is derived from the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) at the receiver, where $\mathsf{SE}_k$ represents the spectral efficiency for user $k$ (in bits/s/Hz), $\mathbf{H}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times N_t}$ is the direct BS-user channel, $\mathbf{G}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_r \times M}$ is the RIS-user channel, $\boldsymbol{\Theta} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times M}$ is the RIS phase-shift matrix, $\mathbf{H}_{BR} \in \mathbb{C}^{M \times N_t}$ is the BS-RIS channel, $\mathbf{f}_k \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ is the beamforming vector for the desired user $k$, $\mathbf{f}_u \in \mathbb{C}^{N_t \times 1}$ are beamforming vectors for interfering users ($u \neq k$), and $\sigma_k^2$ is the noise variance at user $k$ (in watts).
question_text: Which expression correctly represents the argument of the logarithm function (SINR) that should replace [MASK]?
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In the 6G in-X subnetwork system, the Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio (SINR) is derived from RSSI measurements to quantify the communication link quality. Here, $\xi_i^{UL}(m) \in \mathbb{R}^+$ represents the uplink SINR for subnetwork $i$ on channel $m$ (dimensionless), $RSSI_i(m) \in \mathbb{R}^+$ is the total received power measured by subnetwork $i$ on channel $m$ (in dBm), $P_{ik}^{se}(m) \in \mathbb{R}^+$ is the transmission power of sensor $k$ in subnetwork $i$ on channel $m$ (in dBm), $g_{ik}^{UL}(m) \in \mathbb{R}^+$ denotes the intra-subnetwork channel gain from sensor $k$ to controller in subnetwork $i$ on channel $m$ (dimensionless), $\theta_i(m,t) \in \{0,1\}$ is the channel occupation indicator, and $K \in \mathbb{Z}^+$ is the number of sensors per subnetwork.
question_text: Fill in both instances of the desired signal power expression.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: When multiple users transmit simultaneously over shared time-frequency resources, each user’s signal can be impaired by interference from the others and by additive noise. The SINR for user \(k\) balances the desired signal power against interference and noise. The effective channel is \(\boldsymbol{h}_k^H \boldsymbol{G}\). Power \(p_{k'}\) is allocated to user \(k'\), and \(s_{k'}\) is the unit-power symbol for that user.
question_text: Fill in the missing beamforming vector and transmit power in the numerator.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.

background: In a high-SNR MIMO regime, the channel capacity is determined by the available spatial degrees of freedom (eigenmodes). The capacity is given by the sum of the capacities of these parallel channels, where $C$ is the channel capacity (in bits/s/Hz), $R \leq L$ is the rank of the channel Gram matrix $\boldsymbol{G} = \boldsymbol{H}^*\boldsymbol{H}$ (dimensionless), $P_i$ is the power allocated to the $i$-th eigenmode (in watts), $\sigma^2$ is the noise power (in watts), and $\lambda_i$ is the $i$-th largest eigenvalue of $\boldsymbol{G}$ (dimensionless).
question_text: Write the complete expression for the channel capacity in the high-SNR regime.
notes: Real dataset candidate. Needs gold formula, units, valid evidence, and true-but-inapplicable evidence annotation.
